_doc: Chat-mode end-to-end fixture. 22 messages across 3 topics + a multi-user handoff. Run with EVEROS_MEMORIZE__MODE=chat. Expected boundary detector cuts ~3-4 cells (Python bug → weekend plans → cross-team meeting).

messages.content: I'm getting a weird KeyError on a dict comprehension when the source list has None entries. The traceback points at the dict literal, not the comprehension itself.
Can you share a minimal repro? Something like the comprehension expression plus 2-3 sample input items. I want to see whether the None is on the key side or the value side.
Sure: `{r['id']: r['name'] for r in rows}` where rows comes from a left-join and sometimes the right side returns None. So r is None for those rows.

Got it. The None is the row itself, not a field. `None['id']` raises TypeError actually, but if you preprocess the rows to dicts upstream, the missing key on a partial dict would be KeyError. Either way, filter before the comprehension: `{r['id']: r['name'] for r in rows if r is not None and 'id' in r}`.
That worked, thanks. Also occasionally the SQL itself wraps the row in a tuple instead of dict — pyodbc behaviour. Got bitten by it last quarter too.

pyodbc returns Row objects by default; you'd need `cursor.execute(...).fetchall()` then iterate. Or set `cursor.row_factory = lambda c, r: dict(zip([col[0] for col in c.description], r))` to normalise upstream.
Switching topics — any weekend plans? I'm thinking of trying the new ramen place on Carmine Street, supposedly the tonkotsu is decent.
Tonkotsu sounds good. If you go Saturday before 1pm you'll skip the queue. There's also a coffee place two doors down that does decent cold brew if you want a pre-meal stop.
Cold brew yes. Bob mentioned wanting to come too, do you think the ramen place takes reservations for three?
Their site says walk-in only on weekends. For three people the wait is usually 25-40 minutes around noon — manageable but worth knowing.
OK we'll show up at 11:30 then. Will send Bob the address.
Sensible. Reminder it's cash-only for the first 6 months apparently — saw that on a Reddit thread last week.

One more thing — the Q3 revenue review is next Thursday at 2pm, who from engineering needs to attend? I want to make sure platform team is represented since we're presenting the infra cost reductions.
Standard attendees are eng director + relevant TL. For platform cost reductions, your eng lead Carol should be there since she owns the runbook. Add Dan as a backup in case Carol's on PTO.
Good call. Carol mentioned PTO that week actually. Let me ping Dan to confirm he can do the slides handoff.
If Dan has bandwidth, also ask him to pre-share the deck Monday so finance has time to review the cost numbers before the meeting. Saves a lot of meeting time clarifying figures.
Hey, Alice forwarded me about the ramen plan and the Thursday meeting. I can do both but the Thursday slot conflicts with the SRE oncall handoff at 1:45 — anyone we can swap with?
Erin usually does the Wednesday → Thursday handoff for SRE. She owes you one from last cycle. Ask her to take the 1:45 slot and you can ack the comms async at 1:30.
Erin's good. I'll ping her on Slack today. About the ramen — Alice said 11:30 Saturday, that works for me, walking distance from my place.
Great. Just remind Alice it's cash-only for now — she'll forget otherwise based on past experience.
Ha, true. I'll grab some cash on the way. Also can you remind me about the Q3 revenue deck — when does it need to be done by, EOD Tuesday?

Yes — Dan agreed to pre-share Monday, so practical deadline is EOD Tuesday for finance to review Wednesday. After Wednesday review they may ask for last-minute number tweaks; build slack into Thursday morning for that.
